UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the BioLive application. This entity stores public and semi-public user details, excluding sensitive authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique, user-chosen name for their profile.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

MediaItem.title: MediaItem
MediaItem.description: Represents a piece of uploaded content, such as a short video (reel) or a recorded live stream. This entity stores details about the media itself.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaItem entity.

uploaderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this media. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MediaItem)

title.description: The title of the media item.

description.description: A detailed description of the media item.

mediaUrl.description: URL to the actual video content file.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the thumbnail image for the media item.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the media content in seconds.

viewsCount.description: The total number of times this media item has been viewed.

likesCount.description: The total number of likes this media item has received.

commentsCount.description: The total number of comments this media item has received.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the media item was uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the media item was last updated.

LiveStream.title: LiveStream
LiveStream.description: Represents an active or scheduled live broadcast event. This entity tracks the real-time aspects of a live stream.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveStream entity.

broadcasterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is broadcasting the live stream. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LiveStream)

title.description: The title of the live stream.

description.description: A description of the live stream content.

streamUrl.description: The URL for viewers to access the live stream content.

status.description: The current status of the live stream (e.g., 'SCHEDULED', 'LIVE', 'ENDED').

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the live stream officially started.

endedAt.description: Timestamp when the live stream officially ended.

peakViewerCount.description: The highest number of concurrent viewers reached during the live stream.

recordedMediaItemId.description: Optional reference to a MediaItem if this live stream was recorded and saved as VOD. (Relationship: LiveStream 1:1 MediaItem)

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a user comment posted on a MediaItem.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

mediaItemId.description: Reference to the MediaItem this comment belongs to. (Relationship: MediaItem 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who posted the comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

content.description: The textual content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was posted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was last updated (e.g., edited).

parentId.description: Optional reference to another Comment, indicating this is a reply. (Relationship: Comment 1:N Comment)

moderationStatus.description: The moderation status of the comment (e.g., 'APPROVED', 'FLAGGED', 'REMOVED', 'PENDING_REVIEW').

LiveChatMessage.title: LiveChatMessage
LiveChatMessage.description: Represents a single chat message sent during a live stream. These messages may be ephemeral but are stored for moderation and potential history features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveChatMessage entity.

liveStreamId.description: Reference to the LiveStream this message belongs to. (Relationship: LiveStream 1:N LiveChatMessage)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LiveChatMessage)

message.description: The textual content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

moderationStatus.description: The moderation status of the chat message (e.g., 'APPROVED', 'FLAGGED', 'REMOVED').

moderationDetails.description: Additional details about the moderation decision, potentially including AI analysis results (e.g., JSON string).

FollowRelationship.title: FollowRelationship
FollowRelationship.description: Represents a user following another user, establishing a follower-followed relationship.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FollowRelationship entity.

followerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is doing the following. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followedId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is being followed. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the follow relationship was established.

LikeRelationship.title: LikeRelationship
LikeRelationship.description: Represents a user liking a specific MediaItem.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LikeRelationship entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who liked the media item. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N MediaItem)

mediaItemId.description: Reference to the MediaItem that was liked. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N MediaItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the like was recorded.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user, informing them of an event such as new content, followers, or comments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who should receive this notification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Notification)

message.description: The content of the notification message.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the recipient has viewed the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

relatedEntityId.description: Optional reference to the ID of the entity that triggered the notification (e.g., MediaItem ID, UserProfile ID).

relatedEntityType.description: Optional type of the entity that triggered the notification (e.g., 'MediaItem', 'UserProfile', 'LiveStream').

ModerationEvent.title: ModerationEvent
ModerationEvent.description: Records details of a moderation action or event, whether automated by AI or manual, on various content types.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ModerationEvent entity.

targetEntityType.description: The type of entity that was moderated (e.g., 'LiveChatMessage', 'Comment', 'MediaItem', 'UserProfile').

targetEntityId.description: Reference to the ID of the specific entity that was moderated. (Relationship: ModerationEvent 1:1 various entities)

moderatorId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who performed a manual moderation action. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ModerationEvent)

reason.description: The reason for the moderation action (e.g., 'HATE_SPEECH', 'SPAM', 'INAPPROPRIATE_CONTENT', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the moderation event occurred.

metadata.description: Additional JSON string containing context or details, such as AI confidence scores, original content, or links to evidence.

definition.description: Stores a user's profile information. The `userId` in the path MUST match `resource.data.id` and `request.auth.uid` for write operations, ensuring authorization independence for profile ownership.

Dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document here (where document ID is `userId`) grants administrative privileges. This is an existence-based role check, not a full profile storage. References `UserProfile` as the entity whose ID is used for the role.

Stores public short-form videos (reels) and recorded live streams. Includes `uploaderId` in the document for authorization independence; rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.uploaderId`.

Stores active or scheduled live broadcast events. Includes `broadcasterId` in the document for authorization independence; rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.broadcasterId`.
Stores comments for a specific media item. Includes `userId` (commenter) and `mediaItemId` in the document for authorization independence and validation against the path wildcards.
Stores real-time chat messages for a specific live stream. Includes `senderId` and `liveStreamId` in the document for authorization independence and validation against the path wildcards.

Records that `userId` (from path) is following `followedUserId` (from path and document ID). The `followerId` in the document MUST match `userId` from the path and `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence. The `followedId` in the document MUST match `followedUserId` from the path.

Records that `followerUserId` (from path and document ID) is following `userId` (from path). The `followedId` in the document MUST match `userId` from the path for authorization independence. The `followerId` in the document MUST match `followerUserId` from the path.

Records that `userId` (from path) liked `mediaItemId` (from path and document ID). The `userId` in the document MUST match `userId` from the path and `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence. The `mediaItemId` in the document MUST match `mediaItemId` from the path.
Stores private notifications for a specific user. The `recipientId` in the document MUST match `userId` from the path and `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence.
Records all moderation actions, whether manual or AI-driven. Access to this collection is restricted to users with the `adminUsers` role.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to enterprise-level architectural principles. The core philosophy is to minimize rule complexity by making authorization explicit within the data structure itself, rather than relying on complex lookups.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design prioritizes Authorization Independence by consistently denormalizing authorization-critical fields directly into the documents. For instance, `MediaItem` documents include `uploaderId`, `LiveStream` documents include `broadcasterId`, and nested collections like `Comment` and `LiveChatMessage` explicitly contain `userId` (commenter/sender) and their respective parent IDs (`mediaItemId`, `liveStreamId`). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to fetch parent document data for authorization decisions, thus simplifying rules, improving performance, and enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches) without breaking security context. For user-specific data like `FollowRelationship`, `LikeRelationship`, and `Notification`, the `userId` is part of the document's path AND duplicated within the document (`followerId`, `userId`, `recipientId`), ensuring that `request.auth.uid` can be directly compared against both the path wildcard and the document field.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
Collections are designed with a homogeneous security posture to support Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) and simplify rules:
*   `/users/{userId}/...` collections (`following`, `followers`, `likes`, `notifications`) are strictly segregated for private, user-owned data. Only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) matching the `{userId}` wildcard can perform write operations, while read access might be granted to others based on public profile settings (managed by rules based on a hypothetical `UserProfile.isPublic` field, though not explicit in schema, it's a common extension). This makes `list` operations for a user's private data inherently secure.
*   `/mediaItems` and `/liveStreams` are public-readable collections. This enables the 'Mixed Content Feed' feature, where the client application can query these collections directly for all public content. Rules enforce that only the `uploaderId` or `broadcasterId` (denormalized in the document) can modify their respective content. This eliminates server-side filtering and allows efficient client-side content discovery.
*   `/moderationEvents` is a dedicated collection for administrative actions, secured by a Database Access Control (DBAC) mechanism using `/adminUsers/{userId}`. This ensures that moderation data is only accessible to authorized personnel.

**Access Modeling & DBAC:**
*   **Private User Data:** `/users/{userId}` serves as the root for all user-specific, private, or semi-private data using path-based ownership.
*   **User 1:N Entities:** Entities like `MediaItem` and `LiveStream` are stored at the root, referencing the `broadcasterId` or `uploaderId` internally. For relationships where the
